testcases.title: Load cold.

steps.action: Cold-navigate to the Personal Connections page at /settings/connections.

assertions: The page loads at /settings/connections, the Connections heading is visible, Integrations and MCP sections render, and GitLab, self-hosted GitLab, Slack, Linear, and GitHub rows are shown without exposing tokens.

testcases.title: Inspect linked and unlinked provider rows.
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Personal Connections page

steps.action: Inspect each provider row under Integrations and the MCP section.

assertions: Provider names, account labels, Link/Unlink controls, and 'Missing integration/MCP' guidance are readable and associated with the correct provider.

testcases.title: Start OAuth/link flow for each unlinked provider and cancel before authorizing.
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Personal Connections page

steps.action: For GitLab, Slack, Linear, and self-hosted GitLab, click the Link button.
Cancel the flow before completing authorization and return to Personal Connections.

assertions: Each redirect uses the correct provider, a Devin-owned redirect_uri callback, a non-empty state parameter, and no open-redirect or scriptable parameter echo; the account remains unlinked after cancellation.

steps: Return to Personal Connections and verify the provider row still shows No account linked.

testcases.title: Open link flow and test invalid, non-HTTPS, localhost/private-IP, long, Unicode, and injection-like instance URLs.
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Personal Connections page

steps.action: Attempt to start the self-hosted GitLab link flow with malformed or unsafe instance URLs.

assertions: Malformed or unsafe URLs are rejected before request; no SSRF-capable protocol or host is accepted.

executors.blockedReason: The self-hosted GitLab link flow redirects to gitlab.sbx.itsdev.in directly and does not expose a client-side instance-URL input for security injection testing in Playwright.

testcases.title: With approval, unlink a disposable/personal provider account, reload, then re-link.
preconditions: User is authenticated and a disposable linked provider account is available

steps.action: Unlink the disposable provider, reload the page, and re-link it.

assertions: UI and server state stay synchronized; no orphaned token remains; cleanup restores the link.

steps: Re-link the disposable provider to restore the pre-test state.

executors.blockedReason: Requires completing a real external OAuth flow with a disposable account, which is not safe to automate in shared Playwright runs.

testcases.title: Inspect team-enabled MCP connection rows and start/cancel any available OAuth flow.
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Personal Connections page

steps.action: Open the MCP section and inspect any team-enabled MCP rows.
Start and cancel any available MCP OAuth flow.

assertions: MCP descriptions and scopes are clear; OAuth cancellation leaves state unchanged; shared organization identity warnings are visible where applicable.

executors.blockedReason: The QA tenant shows 'No MCPs' with no team-enabled MCP rows available, so there is no OAuth flow to start/cancel in Playwright.

testcases.title: With approval, connect a disposable provider/MCP, start a session that uses it, then disconnect and retest.
preconditions: User is authenticated and a disposable provider/MCP account is available

steps.action: Connect a disposable provider/MCP, use it in a Devin session, then disconnect and retest.

assertions: Connected credentials are available only as documented; disconnect prevents new use without leaking credential values.

executors.blockedReason: Full end-to-end provider authorization and session usage requires real disposable credentials and interactive consent, which is not safe to automate in Playwright.
